title: 10 best herbal teas for indigestion
intro_desc: Herbal teas for indigestion are infusions made from various plants and spices, including chamomile, peppermint, ginger, and fennel. These teas have been used traditionally to alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach discomfort. They work by promoting the production of digestive enzymes, relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract. For example, chamomile tea has calming properties that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate indigestion. Peppermint tea can help relieve gas and bloating, while ginger tea can reduce inflammation and nausea. Fennel tea can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and relieve cramps. By improving digestion, herbal teas can enhance overall health and well-being, allowing individuals to enjoy their meals without worrying about discomfort or pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with indigestion because it contains menthol, a compound that relaxes the gastrointestinal muscles, allowing for the smooth passage of food and reducing the likelihood of indigestion. Additionally, peppermint tea stimulates the flow of bile, which is necessary for fat digestion, and its anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract. Furthermore, peppermint tea has been shown to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain, all of which can contribute to indigestion. Overall, peppermint tea is a natural and effective remedy for promoting healthy digestion and alleviating indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint tea helps relieve indigestion by reducing smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can alleviate symptoms like cramping, bloating, and gas. This is mainly due to the presence of menthol and menthone.

Carminative: Peppermint tea promotes the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, reducing feelings of bloating and discomfort. This is attributed to the oil's ability to increase bile flow and relax the gastrointestinal tissue.
Analgesic: Peppermint tea has pain-relieving properties that can soothe digestive discomfort and pain associated with indigestion, thanks to the presence of menthol.
Antiemetic: Peppermint tea can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of indigestion, due to its calming effects on the gastrointestinal lining.
Cholagogue: Peppermint tea stimulates the production of bile in the liver, which aids in the digestion of fats and the absorption of nutrients, thus improving overall digestion.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the peppermint leaves for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the spent peppermint leaves.
Optionally, add a small amount of honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for indigestion relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves are the most commonly used part of the plant in medicinal teas for indigestion due to their high concentration of menthol and methyl salicylate, which help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and provide digestive relief.
Flowers: Peppermint flowers contain essential oils that can aid in digestion, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas for indigestion.
Seeds: Peppermint seeds are rich in antioxidants and can help soothe the digestive system, making them a useful ingredient in medicinal teas for indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with indigestion because it has carminative properties that allow it to soothe the digestive system and relieve discomfort. The tea's antispasmodic effects help to reduce bloating and gas, making it easier to digest food. Additionally, lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to aid in the breakdown of food, further alleviating indigestion. Furthermore, its calming properties can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can often exacerbate digestive issues. Overall, lemon balm tea is a natural and effective way to soothe an upset stomach and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Lemon balm tea helps to relieve indigestion by reducing bloating and gas, due to its relaxing effect on the gastrointestinal muscles, which is attributed to its volatile oils, including citral and citronellal.
Spasmolytic: Lemon balm tea alleviates indigestion by relaxing the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, thus reducing spasms and cramps, owing to its rosmarinic acid content.
Antiemetic: Lemon balm tea can help manage indigestion by preventing nausea and vomiting, as it contains eugenol, a compound with antiemetic properties.
Cholagogue: Lemon balm tea promotes the flow of bile from the liver, aiding in digestion and the breakdown of fats, due to its polyphenolic compounds, including catechins and flavonoids.
Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea can help soothe inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating indigestion symptoms, thanks to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves with 1 cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's medicinal properties.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Optionally, add honey or lemon juice to taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, for indigestion relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in teas for indigestion due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have antispasmodic effects, making them useful in teas to soothe digestive discomforts.
Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of lemon balm can be included in teas for indigestion, as they contain similar beneficial compounds found in the leaves and flowers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with indigestion because it contains active compounds like bisabolol, matricin, and chamazulene, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and relaxing the smooth muscles in the intestines. This, in turn, can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach cramps. Moreover, chamomile tea has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the breakdown of fats and promotes healthy digestion. Its carminative effect also helps to expel gas from the stomach, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like bisabolol and matricin, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from indigestion symptoms.

Spasmolytic: Chamomile tea has spasmolytic properties due to active constituents like chamazulene, helping to relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, thus alleviating indigestion and associated abdominal pain.
Carminative: German chamomile tea can help expel excess gas from the digestive system, thanks to its carminative properties, reducing bloating, cramping, and other discomforts caused by indigestion.

Antioxidant: Chamomile tea contains antioxidants such as apigenin, luteolin, and quercetin, which can help protect the gastrointestinal lining from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall digestive health and function.

Antibacterial: German chamomile tea exhibits antibacterial properties due to active compounds like α-bisabolol and matricin, which can help combat harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing the risk of indigestion-related infections.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the spent flowers.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to soothe indigestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, making them effective in soothing digestive discomforts.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins and essential oils that can help alleviate inflammation and relax the gastrointestinal muscles, providing relief from indigestion symptoms.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a source of fatty oils and antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, promoting healthy digestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tea helps with indigestion because it contains volatile oils, flavonoids, and other compounds that stimulate the digestive system and promote the production of digestive enzymes. These compounds help to break down fats, proteins, and carbohydrates more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of indigestion, bloating, and other digestive discomforts. Mugwort tea has been used traditionally for centuries as a digestive aid, and its carminative and spasmolytic properties help to soothe the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating cramps, spasms, and other symptoms of indigestion. Drinking mugwort tea after meals can help to improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Mugwort tea helps to expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort that often accompanies indigestion. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as camphor, cineole, and thujone.

Antispasmodic: Mugwort tea helps to relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, alleviating cramps and spasms that can cause pain and discomfort. This is attributed to the plant's flavonoids and essential oils.

Cholagogue: Mugwort tea promotes the production and flow of bile from the liver and gallbladder, aiding in the digestion of fats and the absorption of nutrients. This is due to the presence of lactones, such as costunolide and parthenolide.

Anti-inflammatory: Mugwort tea helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms such as pain, swelling, and redness. This is due to the tea's antioxidant properties and the presence of compounds such as chamazulene.

Diuretic: Mugwort tea helps to increase urine production, flushing toxins and waste products from the body. This can help to reduce the workload on the digestive system and promote overall gut health. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan or kettle.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mugwort herb to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the mugwort herb and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a mug.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for indigestion relief.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Mugwort rhyzomes are commonly used in medicinal teas for indigestion due to their carminative and bitter properties that help to reduce gas and stimulate digestion.
Leaves: The leaves of mugwort are used in teas to alleviate digestive discomfort, as they contain volatile oils that promote the production of digestive enzymes and bile.
Flowers: Mugwort flowers are used in medicinal teas for their antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve abdominal cramps and spasms associated with indigestion.
Seeds: Mugwort seeds are used to make teas that aid in digestion, as they are known to stimulate the appetite, reduce gas, and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with indigestion because it contains volatile oils, such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole, which have antispasmodic properties that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce gas and bloating. Fennel tea also stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which helps break down food in the stomach and speeds up the digestion process. Additionally, fennel tea has a carminative effect, which means it helps prevent the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby reducing discomfort and pain associated with indigestion. Drinking fennel tea after meals can provide relief from symptoms of indigestion and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel tea can help expel gas from the stomach and intestines, providing relief from bloating, cramps, and discomfort. This property is important for indigestion because bloating and cramping can exacerbate symptoms and make digestion less efficient. The active constituents responsible for this carminative effect are volatile oils, including fenchone, anethole, and estragole.

Antispasmodic: Fennel tea can help reduce smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating pain and promoting normal digestion. This property is important for indigestion because spasms can interfere with the digestive process, leading to discomfort and incomplete digestion. The active constituents responsible for this antispasmodic effect are volatile oils, specifically fenchone and estragole.

Choleretic: Fennel tea can stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which aids in fat digestion and absorption. This property is important for indigestion because bile helps break down fats into smaller molecules, making them easier to absorb and utilize by the body. The active constituents responsible for this choleretic effect are volatile oils, particularly anethole and fenchone.

Gastroprotective: Fennel tea can help protect the lining of the stomach and intestines from damage caused by acid, bile, and other irritants. This property is important for indigestion because it can help prevent or alleviate symptoms like heartburn, acid reflux, and gastritis. The active constituents responsible for this gastroprotective effect are volatile oils, such as anethole and limonene, as well as flavonoids like quercetin and rutin.

Diuretic: Fennel tea can promote the elimination of fluids from the body, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract. This property is important for indigestion because it can help alleviate symptoms like bloating and abdominal discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this diuretic effect are volatile oils, including anethole and fenchone, as well as flavonoids like rutin and quercetin.
remedy_recipe: Boil one and a half cups of water in a teapot or saucepan.
Add two teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds to a tea infuser or directly into the boiling water.
Steep the fennel seeds in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed for indigestion relief.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used in medicinal teas for indigestion due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant are used in teas to soothe digestive issues, as they contain volatile oils that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are occasionally included in teas for their mild flavor and gentle support of digestion, making them suitable for those with sensitive stomachs.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tea helps with indigestion because it contains carvol and carvene, two compounds known to stimulate digestive enzymes and relax gastrointestinal muscles. This action helps to alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort. Additionally, caraway tea has antispasmodic properties that can soothe the digestive tract, reducing cramping and pain. Its mild diuretic effect can also help to eliminate excess gas and reduce bloating. Overall, caraway tea is a natural and effective way to promote healthy digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Caraway tea helps to relieve indigestion by reducing bloating and gas, due to the active constituents such as carvol and carvone which help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce muscle spasms.

Antiemetic: Caraway tea can help to alleviate nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of indigestion, as it contains compounds like limonene and alpha-pinene that help to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation.

Cholagogue: Caraway tea promotes the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats, thus helping to improve overall digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils like carvone and limonene.

Antiflatulent: Caraway tea helps to prevent the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the feeling of bloating and discomfort that often accompanies indigestion. This is due to the active constituents like carvone and carvol, which have antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties that help to break down and expel gas.

Stomachic: Caraway tea supports the overall health and function of the stomach, promoting its ability to digest food effectively and alleviating symptoms of indigestion. This is achieved through the presence of various compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and essential oils, which help to strengthen the mucous membrane lining of the stomach and protect it from irritation and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Boil one cup of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried caraway seeds to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the caraway seeds and let them steep for 10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the herbal caraway tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day after meals.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in medicinal teas for indigestion due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Fruits: The dried fruits of the caraway plant are often used in teas for their ability to soothe digestive issues, including indigestion and dyspepsia.
Leaves: Caraway leaves can be used in teas for their anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Rhizomes: Caraway rhizomes contain volatile oils that have been shown to aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and gas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cilantro tea helps with indigestion because it has been traditionally used as a digestive aid due to its carminative properties. The tea is known to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food in the stomach more efficiently. Additionally, cilantro has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion. The tea may also help alleviate gas and bloating, providing relief from symptoms of indigestion. Its calming effect on the digestive system can promote regularity and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Cilantro tea can help expel gas from the stomach, alleviating bloating and discomfort that comes with indigestion, due to its volatile oils, such as borneol and linalool.
Anti-inflammatory property: Cilantro tea can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help soothe irritation and promote healthy digestion, thanks to its flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Anti-spasmodic property: Cilantro tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause pain and discomfort, due to its antispasmodic compounds like cineole and geraniol.

Antibacterial property: Cilantro tea can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, preventing infections and promoting a healthy gut flora, thanks to its antibacterial agents like coriandrol and isocoriandrol.
Diuretic property: Cilantro tea can help eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body, reducing water retention and bloating, due to its diuretic properties that increase urine production.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of fresh cilantro leaves to the boiling water, then reduce heat and simmer for 3 more minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot and let the tea steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove cilantro leaves and discard them.
Drink the warm cilantro tea slowly, up to three times a day, after meals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of cilantro are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating indigestion.
Seeds: Cilantro seeds, also known as coriander, have carminative properties that help in reducing gas and bloating, making them useful for treating indigestion.
Stems: The stems of cilantro contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that aid in digestion by stimulating the production of digestive enzymes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tea helps with indigestion because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and cramping. The carminative effect of spearmint oil found in the tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, allowing for the easy passage of food and gas through the digestive system. Additionally, spearmint tea has been shown to stimulate the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats and can help prevent indigestion. Drinking spearmint tea after meals can provide relief from indigestion and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Spearmint tea helps to relieve indigestion by reducing bloating and gas, thanks to the active constituents such as menthol and carvone which help to relax the gastrointestinal tract.

Antispasmodic: Spearmint tea can help alleviate indigestion caused by abdominal pain and cramping, as it contains rosmarinic acid and flavonoids which help to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract.

Antioxidant: Spearmint tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of inflammation and indigestion, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from indigestion symptoms. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation.

Choleretic: Spearmint tea can help improve digestion by increasing bile production in the liver, aiding in the breakdown and absorption of fats. This is attributed to the active constituent, carvone, which stimulates the production of bile.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried spearmint leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herb's properties.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, ensuring a smooth drink.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid milk or other dairy products.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, as needed for indigestion relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Spearmint leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for indigestion due to their calming and soothing effects on the digestive tract.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds are sometimes included in medicinal teas for indigestion because they contain oils that can help stimulate the digestive system and alleviate symptoms.
Stems: Spearmint stems can be used in medicinal teas for indigestion as they contain similar calming and soothing properties as the leaves.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers are occasionally used in medicinal teas for indigestion as they contain flavonoids that can help relax gastrointestinal muscles and reduce symptoms of indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with indigestion because it contains carvacrol and thymol, two compounds with antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce discomfort. Additionally, oregano tea has been shown to increase the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food more efficiently and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and nausea. Furthermore, the tea's warm temperature can also help stimulate the digestive system and promote regularity. Drinking oregano tea after meals can provide relief from indigestion and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Oregano tea can help to expel gas from the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort that can occur with indigestion. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvacrol and thymol.

Antispasmodic: Oregano tea can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing cramping and pain that can occur with indigestion. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as rosmarinic acid and triterpenes.

Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea can help to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to indigestion symptoms. This property is due to the presence of phenols such as thymol and carvacrol.
Antimicrobial: Oregano tea can help to kill off harmful bacteria in the gut that can contribute to indigestion. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvacrol and thymol.

Choleretic: Oregano tea can help to stimulate the production of bile, which can improve the digestion and absorption of fats and fat-soluble vitamins. This property is due to the presence of compounds such as rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried oregano leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the oregano leaves.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day after meals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Often used in medicinal teas for indigestion due to their carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and gas.
Seeds: Contain oil that aids in digestion and soothing upset stomachs, making them a common addition to tea blends for indigestion relief.
Flowers: High in antioxidants and volatile oils, flowers can help stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
Stems: While less potent than leaves or seeds, stems can still contribute to the soothing effects of medicinal teas for indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with indigestion because it contains active compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing discomfort and promoting digestion. Additionally, thyme tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food more efficiently and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. Furthermore, thyme tea has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe and protect the digestive tract from irritation and infection, providing additional relief from indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Thyme tea helps to relieve indigestion by aiding in the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as thymol and carvacrol.

Antispasmodic: Thyme tea alleviates indigestion by relaxing gastrointestinal muscles, reducing cramps and spasms, and allowing for smoother digestion. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea soothes inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to indigestion symptoms. This is due to the presence of phenolic compounds like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid.
Antimicrobial: Thyme tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, which can lead to indigestion. This is due to the presence of thymol and other volatile oils.
Cholagogue: Thyme tea promotes the production of bile, which aids in the digestion of fats and helps to prevent indigestion. This is due to the presence of saponins and other bitter compounds.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme leaves.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its medicinal properties.
Strain the tea to remove the thyme leaves, pouring the liquid into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your digestive system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antispasmodic and carminative properties, making them ideal for soothing indigestion and reducing gastric discomfort.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain volatile oils that stimulate digestion, alleviate symptoms of indigestion, and promote the secretion of digestive enzymes.
Stems: Thyme stems have similar properties to leaves and flowers, aiding in digestion and reducing symptoms of indigestion, especially when used in tea infusions.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat indigestion with herbal teas is by choosing teas with carminative properties, which help to reduce gas and bloating. Peppermint, ginger, and chamomile teas are excellent choices. Peppermint stimulates the flow of bile, which helps to digest fats more efficiently. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Chamomile contains antispasmodic properties that can help to relax the smooth muscles of the intestines, reducing cramping and discomfort. Drinking these teas after meals can provide relief and promote healthy digestion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to indigestion that are treated with herbal teas include bloating, gas, and nausea. For instance, chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach. Peppermint tea is another popular option, as it can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, providing relief from bloating, gas, and indigestion. Ginger tea is also commonly used to treat nausea, vomiting, and other digestive issues. Additionally, licorice root tea may help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, making it a potential remedy for indigestion and other digestive ailments. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new herbal remedies, especially for those with underlying health conditions or those taking medications.
